Identity.Name: StingPipeMaterials
Identity.Definition: Pipe material codes. Drives BS 1710 pipe-identification colour, embodied-carbon lookup (Pset_StingCarbon via IfcMaterial), compatibility with StingPipeServices (e.g. copper for DCW yes, gas no), and HTM 04-01 water-safety material restrictions for healthcare.

Governance.StandardsBasis: BS EN 806 + BS 1710 (pipe ID); WRAS approved-products scheme; ICE Database v3.0 (embodied carbon)

Value.Definition: Copper — BS EN 1057 R220/R250/R290. Standard for DCW/DHW domestic.
Stainless Steel — 304/316 grades for clean / process / medical / corrosive service.
Galvanised Steel — BS EN 10255 medium- and heavy-weight. Common for sprinkler / general service.
Black Steel — BS EN 10255 / BS EN 10220. Used for gas, heating, steam.
Carbon Steel (general grade) — schedule 40 / 80. Process applications.
Cast Iron — soil/waste underground per BS EN 877.
Ductile Iron — water mains per BS EN 545.
PEX — cross-linked polyethylene, BS EN ISO 15875. Underfloor + domestic hot/cold.
uPVC — unplasticised PVC per BS EN 1452. Cold-water service, soil/waste above ground.
CPVC — chlorinated PVC per BS EN 15493. Higher temperature than PVCu; some sprinkler use.
HDPE — high-density polyethylene per BS EN 12201. Water mains, drainage, syphonic rainwater.
Polypropylene — BS EN ISO 15874. Hot/cold service, lab waste.
ABS — acrylonitrile butadiene styrene per BS EN 1455. Waste / overflow / chilled water.
GRP — glass-reinforced plastic per BS EN 14364. Large-diameter water / drainage.
Lead — heritage / pre-1970 service pipes. DEPRECATED: lead pipework must be replaced under HTM 04-01 / Water Industry Act; carried in enum for legacy survey records only.
